{"parentUuid":"fc793348-9c37-4c6e-863d-56cfa2f25f5a","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YUrZB7gTkiJBAEGnKDTXcu","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Polity V Polity IV dataset territorial data borders geographic\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Polity IV — polityIV • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/polityIV.html\"},{\"title\":\"Polity V: Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions, 1800-2018 - Data and Statistical Services\",\"url\":\"https://dss.princeton.edu/catalog/resource93\"},{\"title\":\"Polity 5 — download_polity_annual • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/download_polity_annual.html\"},{\"title\":\"POLITY™ IV PROJECT Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions, 1800-2016\",\"url\":\"https://www.systemicpeace.org/inscr/p4manualv2016.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"POLITY IV PROJECT Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions, 1800-2004\",\"url\":\"http://www.stevendroper.com/polity.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Polity™ Project POLITY5 Political Regime Characteristics\",\"url\":\"https://www.systemicpeace.org/inscr/p5manualv2018.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"POLITY™ IV PROJECT Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions, 1800-2018\",\"url\":\"https://www.systemicpeace.org/inscr/p4manualv2018.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Find Data & Statistics - Political Science Library Guide - Library Guides at Tulane University\",\"url\":\"https://libguides.tulane.edu/politicalscience/data\"},{\"title\":\"Polity IV Project: Country Reports 2010\",\"url\":\"https://www.systemicpeace.org/polity/polity4.htm\"},{\"title\":\"Polity data series - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polity_data_series\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the Polity V and Polity IV datasets regarding territorial data, borders, and geographic information:\n\n## Overview of the Datasets\n\nPolity V contains coded annual information on regime and authority characteristics for all independent states with greater than 500,000 total population in the global state system. Polity IV Project covers Political Regime Characteristics and Transitions from 1800-2018.\n\n## Territorial and Border Coverage\n\nBoth Polity datasets include some geographic and territorial information, though it's limited. A coverage variable is included with the 1999 Polity updates regarding the territorial coverage of central state authority or the existence of non-state polities within borders. However, no direct inference should be made from the data regarding the effective territorial scope of state authority.\n\n## Key Territorial Variables\n\nPolity Fragmentation codes the operational existence of a separate polity or polities comprising substantial territory and population within the recognized borders of the state over which the coded polity exercises no effective authority. Local autonomy arrangements voluntarily established and accepted by both central and local authorities are not considered fragmentation. A polity that cannot exercise relatively effective authority over at least 50 percent of its established territory is necessarily considered to be in a condition of \"state failure.\"\n\n## Limitations\n\nThe datasets have important limitations regarding borders and geographic data. The state is recognized as the central authority delimited spatially through formal territorial borders, but this does not mean that the authority of the state is coincident with its spatial parameters; distinct areas and populations may be removed from central state control and constitute separate polities outside the legally recognized polity of the state.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"f945f8e8-8969-443a-a863-2d38cb061e9c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:33:50.107Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"fc793348-9c37-4c6e-863d-56cfa2f25f5a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"b0c94575-40d0-483c-90a6-645a9d640a21","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01N56Qn22aybKGmpiTm5VGK2","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Clio-Infra project polities historical data borders territories\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/\"},{\"title\":\"Total Population | Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/Indicators/TotalPopulation.html\"},{\"title\":\"Cliopatria - A geospatial database of world-wide political entities from 3400BCE to 2024CE | Scientific Data\",\"url\":\"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-025-04516-9\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) CLIO World Tables: A Global Historical Database\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/228277231_CLIO_World_Tables_A_Global_Historical_Database\"},{\"title\":\"Polity2 Index | Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/Indicators/Polity2Index.html\"},{\"title\":\"Numeracy (Total) | Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/Indicators/NumeracyTotal.html\"},{\"title\":\"Clio Infra\",\"url\":\"https://datahub.io/@Daniellappv/awesome-data-rrr/notes/clio-infra.eu\"},{\"title\":\"GDP per Capita | Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/Indicators/GDPperCapita.html\"},{\"title\":\"Height | Clio Infra | Reconstructing Global Inequality\",\"url\":\"https://clio-infra.eu/Indicators/Height.html\"},{\"title\":\"Cliopatria - A geospatial database of world-wide political entities from 3400BCE to 2024CE - PMC\",\"url\":\"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11822181/\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, here's what I found about the Clio-Infra project and related databases on historical data about polities, borders, and territories:\n\n## Clio-Infra Project\n\nClio Infra has set up a number of interconnected databases containing worldwide data on social, economic, and institutional indicators for the past five centuries, with special attention to the past 200 years. Economic and social historians from around the world have been working together in thematic collaboratories, in order to collect and share their knowledge concerning the relevant indicators of economic performance and its causes. The collected data have been standardized, harmonized, and stored for future use. The datasets are accessible through the Clio Infra portal which also offers possibilities for visualization of the data.\n\n## Related Historical Polities Databases\n\nWhile the Clio-Infra project focuses on historical socioeconomic indicators, there is a related project called Cliopatria that specifically addresses polities and territories. Cliopatria is a comprehensive open-source geospatial dataset of worldwide states from 3400BCE to 2024CE comprising over 1600 political entities sampled at varying timesteps and spatial scales.\n\n## Important Note on Historical Borders\n\nIt's important to note that the prevalence and magnitude of uncertain border locations of historical entities tends to increase into the past, and the apparent stability of borders of ancient polities over hundreds of years may reflect limited historical records rather than the actual stability of the state itself, which is typical of working with historical data that is often fundamentally uncertain based on the simple paucity of records in addition to differing underlying concepts of border and control.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"740926f4-20c8-4c67-a833-203e2a838886","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:11.594Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b0c94575-40d0-483c-90a6-645a9d640a21","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

T. Graham, Jacob Tucker :: SSRN\",\"url\":\"https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2534067\"},{\"title\":\"International Data and Datasets - International Relations - LibGuides at Michigan State University Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://libguides.lib.msu.edu/c.php?g=96739&p=923034\"},{\"title\":\"Historical International Political Economy - LSE Research Online\",\"url\":\"https://eprints.lse.ac.uk/122429/\"},{\"title\":\"The international political economy data resource | The Review of International Organizations | Springer Nature Link\",\"url\":\"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11558-017-9285-0\"},{\"title\":\"Comparative Politics and International Relations - Data Resources for Political Science and Political Scientists - Research Guides at Emory University Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.libraries.emory.edu/c.php?g=944707&p=6810116\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found information about International Political Economy (IPE) datasets, though the specific \"HIPE\" acronym doesn't appear in these results. However, I can provide you with what I found:\n\n## International Political Economy Data Resources\n\nThe International Political Economy Data Resource seeks to provide a public good to the field by standardizing and merging together 951 variables from 78 core International Political Economy data sources into a single dataset, increasing efficiency and reducing the risk of data management errors.\n\nThe full resource is available via the Harvard Dataverse Network, with versions also available via the Niehaus Center for Globalization and Governance at Princeton University and NewGene.\n\nAdditionally, the World Economics and Politics (WEP) Dataverse is a queryable data resource for comparative and international political economy, developed jointly by the Niehaus Center and the Security and Political Economy (SPEC) Lab at the University of Southern California. The country-year version of the Dataverse includes over 900 variables, covering politics, economics, geography, society, and culture, with data available for more than 200 countries, with observations spanning from 1800 until 2018.\n\nIf you're looking for the specific \"HIPE\" (Historical International Political Economy) dataset, it may be a more specialized or newer resource that wasn't captured in these results. Would you like me to search for more specific information about this dataset?\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"079bd699-f095-4eff-8e46-efb94c0fcd76","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:12.838Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"4b11a515-c5b8-485b-a826-d383d3890ce3","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Dataverse\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/datapass\"}]\n\nThe search results show several relevant resources related to historical polities, borders, and territories in Harvard Dataverse and ICPSR:\n\n## Key Findings:\n\n**ICPSR Harvested Dataverse**: The Harvard Dataverse contains a list of all datasets from the Inter-University Consortium for Political and Social Research (ICPSR).\n\n**Relevant Historical Datasets**:\n\n1. **State Borders and Ethnic Geography**: A study analyzing how ethnic geography and nationalism have shaped European borders since the 19th century, which includes new data on Europe's ethnic geography since 1855.\n\n2. **Colonial Border Studies**: Replication data for research on endogenous colonial borders and precolonial states.\n\n3. **International Border Agreements Dataset**: A dataset focusing on the delimitation of interstate borders under international law, covering the period 1816–2001.\n\n4. **Historical German Territories**: A collection containing locations and border maps for cities of the Holy Roman Empire.\n\n5. **World-Historical Dataverse**: A list of datasets that are currently released in the World-Historical Dataverse, which can be organized by geography, time period, or title.\n\nThese resources appear to be available through the Harvard Dataverse platform, which hosts ICPSR materials and specializes in historical and political data.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown {"parentUuid":"50e63e39-57a8-4e5f-8556-0500a36dd657","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VA3A2iAGpPVgvMDKY9haip","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"World Historical Gazetteer dataset download territories polities\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"World Historical Gazetteer\",\"url\":\"https://whgazetteer.org/public_data/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) World-Historical Gazetteer\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/282397921_World-Historical_Gazetteer\"},{\"title\":\"Introduction — World Historical Gazetteer 0.0.1 documentation\",\"url\":\"https://docs.whgazetteer.org/content/001-Introduction.html\"},{\"title\":\"World-Historical Gazetteer - The Digital Classicist Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.digitalclassicist.org/World-Historical_Gazetteer\"},{\"title\":\"World Historical Gazetteer Dataverse\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/WHG;jsessionid=b2363a61241f80ae0a143d08fd7a\"},{\"title\":\"World Historical Gazetteer\",\"url\":\"https://pleiades.stoa.org/news/blog/world-historical-gazetteer\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) World-Historical Gazetteer Research Report\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/320050872_World-Historical_Gazetteer_Research_Report\"},{\"title\":\"AWOL - The Ancient World Online: World-Historical Gazetteer: Beta Release v. 0.2\",\"url\":\"http://ancientworldonline.blogspot.com/2017/10/coming-soon-world-historical-gazetteer.html\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) World-Historical Gazetteer\",\"url\":\"https://www.academia.edu/79775388/World_Historical_Gazetteer\"},{\"title\":\"Finding Places in Text with the World Historical Gazetteer | Programming Historian\",\"url\":\"https://programminghistorian.org/en/lessons/finding-places-world-historical-gazetteer\"}]\n\nI've found comprehensive information about the World Historical Gazetteer dataset. Here are the key findings:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe World Historical Gazetteer (WHG) is a project directed by Dr. Ruth Mostern of the University of Pittsburgh World History Center, which aims to provide a collection of content and services that permit world historians to do spatial and temporal reasoning and visualization in a data rich environment at global and trans-regional scales.\n\n## Data Access and Download\n\nAll public data are made available under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) License, and most are downloadable from their linked page. WHG visitors can search across more than 2 million place records and browse over 70 published datasets and collections.\n\nA dataverse containing data associated with the WHG project is available at Harvard Dataverse.\n\n## Dataset Contents\n\nThe Spine will be a broad-but-shallow gazetteer of place names for all continents and regions and all centuries since 1500, including names of polities, districts and significant settlements; and ethnonyms, language names, and names of physical features.\n\nThe index has been instantiated with records from modern gazetteer resources including about 1,000 of the world's most populous cities from GeoNames, ~1.8 million place records from Getty TGN, about 1,500 societies from the D-Place anthropological repository, and major rivers, lakes, and mountain ranges from Natural Earth and Wildlife Research Institute. Historical data includes 10,600 entities from the Atlas of World History and HGIS de las Indias, which consists of approximately 15,000 settlements and territories in colonial Latin America.\n\n## File Formats\n\nThe data format currently adopted by the WHG is Linked Places.\n\nFor more detailed information and to download datasets, visit the official WHG website at https://whgazetteer.org/public_data/\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"020d9f7e-efe6-442c-a6e1-c1088feb310c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:14.815Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"50e63e39-57a8-4e5f-8556-0500a36dd657","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"aeab897d-f532-4e67-b2f9-dca1bc2066dc","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014Fkz8qW8oE2BDe5nFo9Uze","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Max Planck Institute historical borders territories geospatial dataset\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Historical dataset of administrative units with social-economic attributes for Austrian Silesia 1837–1910 | Scientific Data\",\"url\":\"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-020-0546-z\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic data files | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/mosaic-data-files\"},{\"title\":\"Geographies of Knowing: China Historical GIS | MPIWG\",\"url\":\"https://www.mpiwg-berlin.mpg.de/research/projects/NWG_Schaefer01_6-2-2\"},{\"title\":\"Max Planck Institute of Geoanthropology Homepage\",\"url\":\"https://www.gea.mpg.de/\"},{\"title\":\"Software and Datasets - Max Planck Institute for Informatics\",\"url\":\"https://www.mpi-inf.mpg.de/departments/computer-vision-and-machine-learning/software-and-datasets\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Mosaic: recovering surviving census records and reconstructing the familial history of Europe\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/273508116_Mosaic_recovering_surviving_census_records_and_reconstructing_the_familial_history_of_Europe\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles\"},{\"title\":\"Home Page | MPIWG\",\"url\":\"https://www.mpiwg-berlin.mpg.de/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Max Planck Institute historical borders and territories geospatial datasets:\n\n## MPIDR Population History GIS Collection\n\nThe MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national and regional administrative boundaries and historical place names.\n\nThe collection includes several important datasets:\n\n**European Administrative Boundaries**: The collection provides information on the administrative division of European states at varying geographic detail in 30-year intervals (1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003).\n\n**German Empire Data**: It provides annual information on the statistical units of the German Empire at the level of small administrative areas (kleine Verwaltungsbezirke). It also provides annual information on the administrative division of the German Union at varying geographic detail across states and time.\n\n**Regional Data**: The collection includes administrative division data for Albania at the municipality level in 1918, covering the northern and central part of present-day Albania which had been occupied by the Austro-Hungarian Empire during World War I, with census results that can be linked to the shapefile.\n\nShapefiles created by Helmut Rumpler and Martin Seger were slightly modified for the Max Planck Institute for Demographic Research and Chair for Geodesy and Geoinformatics, University of Rostock Population History GIS Collection.\n\nThese datasets are designed to support historical demographic and socioeconomic research by providing geographic boundaries for historical administrative units across Europe.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"6c2f2fa7-7738-481f-9443-ccdaa5c308ef","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:26.810Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"aeab897d-f532-4e67-b2f9-dca1bc2066dc","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"e21a20b2-c464-4ac4-86a0-089ba4da562d","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JeWAPiMm4YSes6qqgfGRvj","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"UN World Bank historical country codes date ranges ISO 3166 dataset\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Comprehensive country codes: ISO 3166, ITU, ISO 4217 currency codes and many more\",\"url\":\"https://datahub.io/core/country-codes\"},{\"title\":\"World maps with ISO Alpha 2 & 3 codes for each country – Data Viz Collective\",\"url\":\"https://aditya-dahiya.github.io/projects_presentations/data_vizs/tidy_iso_country_codes.html\"},{\"title\":\"Country-Year State System Coding — country_year_coder • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/country_year_coder.html\"},{\"title\":\"Country Metadata - WITS - World Bank\",\"url\":\"https://wits.worldbank.org/countryprofile/metadata/en/country/all\"},{\"title\":\"Database with different country code schemes and world regions — gisco_countrycode • giscoR\",\"url\":\"https://ropengov.github.io/giscoR/reference/gisco_countrycode.html\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes: ISO 3166-1 country lists merged with their UN Geoscheme regional codes in ready-to-use JSON, XML, CSV data sets · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes\"},{\"title\":\"ISO - ISO 3166 — Country Codes\",\"url\":\"https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html\"},{\"title\":\"Country Codes (ISO 3166)\",\"url\":\"https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/wbdill/country-codes-iso-3166\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - datasets/country-codes: Comprehensive country code information, including ISO 3166 codes, ITU dialing codes, ISO 4217 currency codes, and many others · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/datasets/country-codes\"},{\"title\":\"iso3166-2 · PyPI\",\"url\":\"https://pypi.org/project/iso3166-2/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about UN/World Bank historical country codes and ISO 3166 datasets:\n\n## Key Resources\n\n**Comprehensive Country Code Datasets:**\nComprehensive country code information is available including ISO 3166 codes, ITU dialing codes, ISO 4217 currency codes, with official names from United Nations Protocol and Liaison Service. These are available at datahub.io/core/country-codes.\n\n**Historical and Former Country Codes:**\nISO 3166 standard assigns codes to countries, their subdivisions, and former territories, facilitating exploration of details on formerly recognized countries and their withdrawal dates.\n\n**World Bank Integration:**\nRegions are defined in the World Bank Development Indicators and continents as defined by World Bank Development Indicators are available through various country code systems.\n\n**ISO 3166 Standard Structure:**\nISO 3166 has three parts: codes for countries, codes for subdivisions and formerly used codes, with country codes represented as a two-letter code (alpha-2), a three-letter code (alpha-3) or a three-digit numeric code.\n\n**UN Regional Classifications:**\nData from Wikipedia ISO 3166-1 article and UN M49 Standard country or area codes for statistical use provide countries' regional and sub-regional codes.\n\n## Additional Tools\nWorld Bank WITS provides metadata information like name, country code, income group, region, and currency for all countries.\n\nThese resources offer various formats (JSON, CSV, XML) and combine multiple coding systems, including UN M49 codes alongside ISO 3166 country codes.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"385f893b-7e63-4dd8-94ff-e0694a8b87c4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:27.635Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e21a20b2-c464-4ac4-86a0-089ba4da562d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"d568ff18-cb71-45fa-838b-85ceaf2ac053","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B7GoBAV4M5HsKoHwA9Xyn9","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Zenodo historical polities borders geospatial dataset\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Cliopatria - A geospatial database of world-wide political entities from 3400BCE to 2024CE | Scientific Data\",\"url\":\"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-025-04516-9\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - Seshat-Global-History-Databank/cliopatria: Cliopatria is a comprehensive open-source geospatial dataset of worldwide states, political groups, events, and rulers from 3400BCE to 2024CE. It is part of the Seshat Global History Databank project.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/Seshat-Global-History-Databank/cliopatria\"},{\"title\":\"Modern China Geospatial Database - Main Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://zenodo.org/records/8063584\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"Geospatial Dataset of GNSS Anomalies and Political Violence Events\",\"url\":\"https://zenodo.org/records/15665065\"},{\"title\":\"Historical City Maps Semantic Segmentation Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://zenodo.org/records/5513639\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data and Maps - Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"},{\"title\":\"Zenodo\",\"url\":\"https://zenodo.org/\"},{\"title\":\"Free GIS Datasets - Categorised List\",\"url\":\"https://freegisdata.rtwilson.com/\"},{\"title\":\"From Historical OpenStreetMap data to customized training samples for geospatial machine learning\",\"url\":\"https://zenodo.org/records/3923040\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found regarding historical polities borders geospatial datasets on Zenodo:\n\n## Cliopatria Dataset\n\nThe most prominent result is Cliopatria, a comprehensive open-source geospatial dataset of worldwide states from 3400BCE to 2024CE, comprising over 1600 political entities sampled at varying timesteps and spatial scales.\n\nThe Cliopatria dataset is publicly available in a Zenodo repository and is distributed as a single data file, 'cliopatria.geojson', consisting of approximately 15,000 records.\n\nData for each entity (e.g., 'Roman Empire') is contained in one or more rows, depending on how the associated data about the entity changes.\n\n## Key Features\n\nOne of the primary motivations of compiling the Cliopatria dataset and providing it as open-source material is to foster productive, collaborative dialogue with other users and makers of historical geo-spatial information.\n\nIt is part of the Seshat Global History Databank project, and you can explore the Cliopatria dataset in an interactive Jupyter notebook, which includes a processing script to add colors to the dataset and a notebook that loads the data in GeoPandas with an interactive Folium plot.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e1c260cc-a40a-40dc-bec7-48e26237c089","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:28.625Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d568ff18-cb71-45fa-838b-85ceaf2ac053","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"ce1e263c-b66c-4c80-a7a9-d515644eceed","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QRhMV45Dmnx6A2qwVgujxY","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"historical atlas structured data GIS borders shapefile download 1860 1960\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Download U.S. Files | Atlas of Historical County Boundaries Project\",\"url\":\"https://publications.newberry.org/ahcb/downloads/united_states.html\"},{\"title\":\"Historical - GIS Data Sources - LibGuides at Duke University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.duke.edu/gisdata/gisdata_historical\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data and Maps - Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Resources Thread | alternatehistory.com\",\"url\":\"https://www.alternatehistory.com/forum/threads/historical-gis-resources-thread.516971/\"},{\"title\":\"Resources for Mapping US Boundaries Over Time | Lincoln Mullen\",\"url\":\"https://lincolnmullen.com/blog/resources-for-mapping-us-boundaries-over-time/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data - GIS Data - Research Guides at Dartmouth College\",\"url\":\"https://researchguides.dartmouth.edu/gisdata/historicalgisdata\"},{\"title\":\"Where to Find GIS Data for Historical Country Boundaries - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/find-gis-data-historical-country-boundaries/\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Research Network\",\"url\":\"http://www.hgis.org.uk/resources.htm\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are the key resources for finding historical atlas structured data, GIS borders, and shapefile downloads for the 1860-1960 period:\n\n## Major Resources\n\n**Atlas of Historical County Boundaries** - The single most useful resource is the Atlas of Historical County Boundaries from the Newberry Library, which includes boundary files for states and territories from 1783 to 2000, and also for counties from 1629 to 2000. The boundaries are available as both shapefiles and KMZ files suitable for Google Earth. You can access this at https://digital.newberry.org/ahcb/\n\n**National Historical Geographic Information System (NHGIS)** - The NHGIS offers state- and county-level shapefiles for each decade since 1790. This resource covers the entire 1860-1960 period you're interested in.\n\n**International Historical Geographic Information System (IHGIS)** - The International Historical Geographic Information System provides data tables from population and housing censuses as well as agricultural censuses from around the world, along with corresponding GIS boundary files and shapefiles to describe the boundaries of administrative and statistical units.\n\n## Additional European Resources\n\nEuropean administrative division data is available at varying geographic detail in 30-years intervals (1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003). For Prussia (1860-1870), geocodes are based on Patrick Galloway's collection of Prussian statistical data.\n\n\"A Vision of Britain through Time\" from the University of Portsmouth has made historical boundary information for counties, parishes, and constituencies for England, Wales, and Scotland available in shapefiles format, covering a variety of timeframes in the 1800s and 1900s.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"7049252d-810a-4c41-8ad3-371c6f2e5c0d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:29.341Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ce1e263c-b66c-4c80-a7a9-d515644eceed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

aourednik/historical-basemaps\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps/labels\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the aourednik/historical-basemaps GitHub project:\n\n## Project Overview\n\nThe historical-basemaps is a collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale. This historical boundaries project aims at providing ready-to-use base maps for mapping historical data.\n\n## Key Features\n\n- **Data Format**: All maps are stored in the geojson format: single file per feature layer, human and machine readable, easy to import in qGIS or use in D3.\n\n- **Projection**: The geodata are stored in the WGS 84 projection, EPSG:4326 (crs:OGC:1.3:CRS84). Coordinates are in LatLon, the projection is geographical.\n\n## Important Note\n\nIt is work in progress: verify the maps by comparison to other sources before using in academic work.\n\n## Applications\n\nSeveral apps using the historical-basemaps dataset have been developped. A non-exhaustive list: Historic Borders: a web app with timeline, by Adam (GitHub user @ngrapple). Point in History: a web app to visualize the history of any point on the planet by Hans Hack.\n\nThe project is open to contributions, and the repository is available at https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"fc38bdfd-f1f1-4342-80f7-a870bdf7841a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:42.830Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d96e7b75-5e1d-45ef-8bcc-dd34e5cf0134","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Owsiak Department of International Affairs\",\"url\":\"http://andrewowsiak.org/uploads/2/6/1/1/26111381/owsiak_-_cv__08-2020.pdf\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about the International Border Agreements Dataset created by Andrew P. Owsiak:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe International Border Agreements Dataset (IBAD) focuses on the delimitation of interstate borders under international law. The dataset contains information on the agents involved (e.g. states, third-parties, and colonial powers), methods used during (e.g. negotiation, mediation, arbitration, adjudication, administrative decrees, post-war conferences, and plebiscites), and outcomes of (e.g. full and intermediate agreements) the border settlement process during the period 1816–2001.\n\n## Scope and Use\n\nThe focus on international legal agreements and the process that produces them makes the IBAD valuable for those that study not only territorial conflict, but also international conflict, cooperation, law, and conflict management.\n\n## Citation and Access\n\nThe dataset was authored by Owsiak, Andrew P., Allison K. Cuttner, and Brent Buck, and published in 2016 in Conflict Management and Peace Science. The dataset contains information on whether neighboring states have delimited the entirety of their mutual border(s) under international law.\n\nThe dataset is publicly available through Andrew P. Owsiak's website and through Harvard Dataverse for researchers who wish to use it in their own work studying territorial changes and border disputes.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"a2585f02-fd20-47bb-8af9-44f7ce304677","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:43.842Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9b0db5a6-cd84-4b52-b931-1395acf39230","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

1700\",\"url\":\"https://www.euratlas.net/shop/maps_gis/gis_1700.html\"}]\n\nHere are the search results for Euratlas historical map Europe boundaries shapefile GIS download:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe Euratlas Historical Political Boundaries of Europe vector data describe the political situation in Europe at the turn of each century A.D. The map covers Europe from 15 degrees west to 50 degrees east, 20 degrees north to 60 degrees north.\n\n## Data Format and Access\n\nThese maps have been improved and redrawn in 2008 and are available as shapefiles data which can be analyzed and organized spatially with a GIS software. They describe the political situation of this area at the first day of each centennial year C.E. from year 1 to 2000.\n\n## Download Options\n\nThere are two main sources for obtaining this data:\n\n1. **Euratlas Shop** - Pay and you will receive a web-link, valid 1 week, with login and password to download the data as a ZIP file. Payment is possible by credit card, PayPal, bank transfer or cheque.\n\n2. **Duke University Libraries** - These data are available to current Duke University students, faculty and staff for the seven centuries listed below. A Duke University library resource page is available at https://library.duke.edu/data/sources/euratlas.\n\n## Data Layers\n\nEach century consists of several layers: 1) Autonomous peoples: populations with no well-defined authority. 2) Cities: with name and size information for the current century. big_rivers = main european rivers, fuzzy_borders = polygons representing the uncertainty of some countries boundaries, rivers = main european rivers useful for historical cartography, seas = seas and main lakes.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"9a690b0e-f5a8-4e3a-97b6-a94f007bbb25","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:44.501Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"80433949-b0a5-488f-93ca-b264abef55f5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"678a19d5-93cb-4ed0-8e22-6aa18a7c978e","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HZopY1xvr7buL12DYvQYMK","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"IHGIS International Historical Geographic Information System IPUMS census boundaries\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"IPUMS IHGIS | International Historical Geographic Information System\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS NHGIS | National Historical Geographic Information System\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/\"},{\"title\":\"About IPUMS IHGIS | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/about-ipums-ihgis\"},{\"title\":\"Geography & GIS | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/geography-gis\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS IHGIS | IPUMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.ipums.org/projects/ipums-ihgis\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS NHGIS | IPUMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.ipums.org/projects/ipums-nhgis\"},{\"title\":\"D050.V15.0 | IPUMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.ipums.org/projects/ipums-nhgis/d050.v15.0\"},{\"title\":\"D050.V17.0 | IPUMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.ipums.org/projects/ipums-nhgis/d050.V17.0\"},{\"title\":\"D050.V13.0 | IPUMS\",\"url\":\"https://www.ipums.org/projects/ipums-nhgis/d050.v13.0\"},{\"title\":\"Dataset Descriptions | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/dataset-descriptions\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about IHGIS, IPUMS, and census boundaries:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe International Historical Geographic Information System (IHGIS) provides data tables from population and housing censuses as well as agricultural censuses from around the world, along with corresponding GIS boundary files.\n\n## Data and Services\n\nThe data in IHGIS were originally published by national statistical offices of the respective countries, primarily in PDF documents. IHGIS extracts the data from the documents and provides them in a standardized, machine-readable structure.\n\nThrough the IHGIS Data Finder, users may select and download data tables from one or more census datasets, potentially covering multiple countries, years, or census types. Shapefiles representing the units described in the tables are available for download on the Geography & GIS page.\n\n## GIS Boundary Features\n\nThe international boundaries of all shapefiles have been adjusted to align with the Large Scale International Boundaries (LSIB) dataset from the U.S. Department of State. You can therefore map neighboring countries with no gaps or overlaps along their shared border.\n\nIHGIS is part of the larger IPUMS (Integrated Public Use Microdata Series) family of projects, which also includes NHGIS (National Historical Geographic Information System) that focuses specifically on U.S. census data and boundaries.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"7552c8e0-1da4-4e22-a979-429e5236c6e0","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:58.669Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"678a19d5-93cb-4ed0-8e22-6aa18a7c978e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"f90b1b57-052b-4b9a-956a-6f202df5190e","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WtYwhW88VRiMFsnrvBt67L","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"GeaCron historical atlas world map geospatial API data download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"World History Maps & Timelines | GeaCron World History Atlas since 3000 BC\",\"url\":\"http://geacron.com/home-en/\"},{\"title\":\"new2 | GeaCron World History Atlas since 3000 BC\",\"url\":\"http://geacron.com/new2/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"GeaCron History Maps\",\"url\":\"https://geacron.andro.io/\"},{\"title\":\"The Geacron Project | GeaCron World History Atlas since 3000 BC\",\"url\":\"http://geacron.com/the-geacron-project/\"},{\"title\":\"GeaCron - Historical Interactive World Atlas - Projects - DAIHUM: Digital & AI Humanities\",\"url\":\"https://ai-humanities.com/t/geacron-historical-interactive-world-atlas/908\"},{\"title\":\"GeaCron History Maps 55.0 Free Download\",\"url\":\"https://geacron-history-maps.soft112.com/\"},{\"title\":\"more_historical_info | GeaCron World History Atlas since 3000 BC\",\"url\":\"https://geacron.com/more_historical_info/\"},{\"title\":\"GeaCron History Maps - Download\",\"url\":\"https://geacron-history-maps.updatestar.com/en\"},{\"title\":\"GeaCron - World History Atlas & Timelines | Madrid | Facebook\",\"url\":\"https://www.facebook.com/GeaCron/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about GeaCron:\n\n## Overview\nGeaCron History Maps is a web-based application developed by GEACRON that allows users to explore world history through interactive maps. It provides information by year since 3000 BC, offering Universal History datasets and relevant Universal History vector information.\n\n## Features\nThe platform enables users to visualize changes in borders, empires, and nations over time. Users can zoom in and out on maps for granular exploration of historical regions, and a robust search feature enables users to find specific events or areas efficiently.\n\n## Data Access Limitations\nIt's worth noting that the tool offers no possibility to extract polygons for use in a GIS, which means geospatial data download capabilities appear to be limited. The search results don't indicate the availability of a dedicated geospatial API or direct data download functionality for developers.\n\n## Availability\nGeaCron is based in Madrid, Spain. The application is available through both web and mobile platforms, though recent information suggests changes to premium offerings.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e2cede4c-64f6-4a8f-be5f-5bc373e9d043","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:34:59.922Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f90b1b57-052b-4b9a-956a-6f202df5190e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"d96ad16b-c6a3-4a92-b70c-5a8187aff6cd","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017nfHo6C3W4mhN6EJZ5bRvs","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"TerritorialChanges dataset state formation dissolution 1816 shapefile\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Territorial Change (v6) – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/territorial-change/\"},{\"title\":\"Full article: The Geography of the International System: The CShapes Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/03050620903554614\"},{\"title\":\"Data Sets – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/\"},{\"title\":\"Resources for Mapping US Boundaries Over Time | Lincoln Mullen\",\"url\":\"https://lincolnmullen.com/blog/resources-for-mapping-us-boundaries-over-time/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Territorial Changes, 1816–1996: Procedures and Data\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/247713936_Territorial_Changes_1816-1996_Procedures_and_Data\"},{\"title\":\"1 The From Empire to Nation-State dataset Andreas Wimmer and Brian Min UCLA\",\"url\":\"http://www.columbia.edu/~aw2951/EmpireNSdataset.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Administrative Boundaries - GIS Data - Research Guides at Brandeis University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.brandeis.edu/c.php?g=990410&p=7166514\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data and Maps - Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the TerritorialChanges dataset and related resources:\n\n## Territorial Change Dataset\n\nThe Territorial Change dataset contains information on the entities involved, the gaining side, the losing side, the territory exchanged, as well as the type and date of the exchange, with the sixth version released on March 25, 2019 covering 1816-2018. The revised territorial change data – version 6 – is available in .csv format.\n\nThe Territorial Change Dataset (Tir, Diehl, and Goertz 1998) lists all territorial transfers since 1816 that involved at least one independent state.\n\n## CShapes Dataset with Shapefile Support\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019 and from 1816 for CShapes-Europe. The latest version (2.0) of the raw CShapes data is available directly in CSV (UTF-8 encoded), GeoJSON, Shapefile, SQL, and R package formats.\n\nTo code changes due to the creation and dissolution of units, the coding relies on the GW and COW lists of states and the COW list of dependencies, which track the historical lifespans of political units.\n\nFor accessing these datasets:\n- The Territorial Change dataset is available through The Correlates of War Project\n- CShapes data is available from ETH (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology)\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"ca2d11b3-80ae-4b13-917d-4fbcedb2a9fc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:00.787Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d96ad16b-c6a3-4a92-b70c-5a8187aff6cd","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"93897029-f014-4376-a04d-14e5e5bd1e3d","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019bw4NHUSTGQ7rmY3qNGL4v","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"HGIS de las Indias historical geographic information system colonial territories\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"HGIS of Indias, a historic SIG of colonial America - GEHC\",\"url\":\"https://www.ub.edu/gehc/en/hgis-of-indias-a-historic-sig-of-colonial-america/\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias – digital humanities im deutschsprachigen raum\",\"url\":\"https://digitalhumanities.de/en/projekt/hgis-de-las-indias/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Stangl, Werner, dir., Sistema de información histórico-geográfica (HGIS) de las Indias (1701-1808) | Maria José Afanador-Llach - Academia.edu\",\"url\":\"https://www.academia.edu/84187583/Stangl_Werner_dir_Sistema_de_informaci%C3%B3n_hist%C3%B3rico_geogr%C3%A1fica_HGIS_de_las_Indias_1701_1808_?uc-sb-sw=53661361\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias | Economic Growth Center\",\"url\":\"https://egc.yale.edu/bridging-atlantic-migrations-and-their-legacies/resources\"},{\"title\":\"World Historical Gazetteer\",\"url\":\"https://whgazetteer.org/datasets/15/places\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/hgis-indias\"},{\"title\":\"Digital Resources: HGIS de las Indias | Oxford Research Encyclopedia of Latin American History\",\"url\":\"https://oxfordre.com/latinamericanhistory/display/10.1093/acrefore/9780199366439.001.0001/acrefore-9780199366439-e-822\"},{\"title\":\"Interactive Maps\",\"url\":\"https://www.hgis-indias.net/index.php/en/webgis\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indas - A Historical-Geographic Information System for Spanish America (1701-1808) | CLARIAH-AT\",\"url\":\"https://clariah.at/en/projects/hgis-de-las-indas-historical-geographic-information-system-spanish-america-1701-1808/\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias\",\"url\":\"https://hgis-indias.net/index.php/en/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's comprehensive information about HGIS de las Indias:\n\n## Overview\n\nHGIS de las Indias is a historical-geographical information system for late colonial Spanish America (1701-1808). It is currently maintained by Yale University's Economic Growth Center, and in its origin was a research project funded by the Austrian Scientific Fund (FWF) from 2015 to 2019 and located at University of Graz.\n\n## Core Features\n\nIts core is an interactive WebGIS that allows visualization of various layers of information on colonial history year by year via a timeline. Key elements include territorial units (such as viceroyalties, judicial districts, provinces, dioceses...) as areas and boundaries; settlements visualizable by different criteria (settlement type, ecclesiastical hierarchy...); communication infrastructure (postal routes); \"extracolonial\" elements (contract lines, claims of foreign powers, autonomous indigenous peoples, internal and external conflict); historical natural events (earthquakes, volcanic eruptions); and other aggregated data from various fields such as demography, economy, and culture.\n\n## Data and Resources\n\nThe system includes a gazetteer of 12000+ populated places and 1000+ territorial entities designed to enable integration of third-party data. The datasets include civil and church administrative entities at various levels, settlement types, missionary towns, ports, wharfs, demographic data, data on postal routes, centers of fiscal and other administration, silver registries, printing presses, data on mining mita, and indigenous groups.\n\n## Accessibility and Collaboration\n\nThe project targets a broad, Spanish-speaking audience (via the web application, wiki resource), colleagues with GIS user skills (by providing vector data), and those without GIS user skills (through the automated workflow and web application). HGIS de las Indias is a key component of ARCA (Archive and Resource Center for the Americas), a collaborative initiative aimed at uniting diverse datasets and resources, building a data landscape for the field.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"9da28264-1d42-45e6-96e4-adb987facdf4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:02.721Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"93897029-f014-4376-a04d-14e5e5bd1e3d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"b3ca1072-c067-4f37-841e-47091916cb16","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GjnhxnQDZLqS9VNhPgCBEP","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"\"From Empire to Nation-State\" dataset Wimmer Min territorial boundaries\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"1 The From Empire to Nation-State dataset Andreas Wimmer and Brian Min UCLA\",\"url\":\"http://www.columbia.edu/~aw2951/EmpireNSdataset.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"DEVECONDATA: The From Empire to Nation-State dataset\",\"url\":\"https://devecondata.blogspot.com/2008/09/from-empire-to-nation-state-dataset.html?m=1\"},{\"title\":\"From Empire to Nation-State: Explaining Wars in the Modern World, 1816–2001 - Andreas Wimmer, Brian Min, 2006\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/000312240607100601\"},{\"title\":\"Untitled Document\",\"url\":\"https://www.columbia.edu/~aw2951/Datasets.html\"},{\"title\":\"Data | Andreas Wimmer\",\"url\":\"https://www.awimmer.com/data\"},{\"title\":\"Data | OpenScholar @ Princeton\",\"url\":\"https://scholar.princeton.edu/awimmer/pages/data\"},{\"title\":\"Summary of\\\" Waves of War: Nationalism, State Formation, and Ethnic Exclusion in the Modern World\\\" | Beyond Intractability\",\"url\":\"https://www.beyondintractability.org/bksum/wimmer-waves\"},{\"title\":\"Articles to download\",\"url\":\"https://www.awimmer.com/articles\"},{\"title\":\"1 Nationalism’s rise to power across the world.\",\"url\":\"https://www.asanet.org/wp-content/uploads/savvy/sectionchs/documents/WimmerFeinstein.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"The Rise of the Nation-State across the World, 1816 to 2001 - Andreas Wimmer, Yuval Feinstein, 2010\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0003122410382639\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here is information about the \"From Empire to Nation-State\" dataset by Wimmer and Min:\n\n## Dataset Overview\n\nThe From Empire to Nation-State dataset was constructed by Andreas Wimmer and Brian Min at UCLA as a cross-\"territory\" panel dataset from 1816 to 2001, where \"territory\" refers to a geographical unit with national boundaries in 2001, and includes the whole history of today's nations since 1816, including years before independence.\n\n## Territorial Boundaries\n\nThe dataset records the outbreak of war in fixed geographic territories from 1816 to 2001, independent of the political entity in control of a territory. The dataset uses the 2001 grid of states to determine the units of observation.\n\n## Dataset Scope and Coverage\n\nThe dataset covers 140 territories (in their post-independence geographic extension) and years from 1816 onward. The dataset includes 156 territorial units, 140 of which were incorporated into an empire at some point (92 between 1816 and 2001), and 150 of which experienced nation-state creation.\n\n## Variables Included\n\nVariables include the incidence and type of wars, political institutions, GDP, population, oil production per capita, etc.\n\n## Research Focus\n\nThe authors argue that the change in the institutional form of states is itself a major cause for war, and the rise of empires and the global spread of the nation-state are the most important institutional transformations in the modern age. Analysis of this data set demonstrates that wars are much more likely during and because of these two transformations.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"bfa4fa4f-ae4b-46d2-a69f-d8ab0197c6b3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:15.956Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b3ca1072-c067-4f37-841e-47091916cb16","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"b7eb8b2d-96c5-4b49-9b74-0a98d77fb84b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DAr4kkiXPmmiPprz5gDuAH","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Weidmann Gleditsch territorial conflict dataset polities geospatial 2010 2020\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Full article: The Geography of the International System: The CShapes Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/03050620903554614\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/03050620903554614\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Ethnic Power Relations (EPR) Dataset Family\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping and Measuring Country Shapes\",\"url\":\"https://journal.r-project.org/articles/RJ-2010-004/index.html\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Data\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/\"},{\"title\":\"[PDF] Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset | Semantic Scholar\",\"url\":\"https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Mapping-the-International-System,-1886-2019:-The-Schvitz-Girardin/a13fa3285df251d88629539fe9a62ac85c37ea2d\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Research Repository\",\"url\":\"https://repository.essex.ac.uk/31791/\"},{\"title\":\"GIS and Territorial Disputes - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/gis-territorial/\"}]\n\n## Search Results Summary\n\nBased on the web search for information about Weidmann, Gleditsch, territorial conflict datasets, geospatial data, and the 2010-2020 period, here are the key findings:\n\n### CShapes Dataset\n\nThe foundational work by Weidmann, Kuse, and Gleditsch (2010) created the original CShapes dataset, which covers independent states from 1946 onward. CShapes provides historical maps of state boundaries and capitals in the post-World War II period.\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019, and there are two versions of the dataset, which are based on the Gleditsch and Ward (1999) or the Correlates of War coding of independent states.\n\n### GeoEPR Dataset\n\nRelated to geospatial conflict research, the GeoEPR dataset provides geo-spatial information about every politically relevant ethnic group and assigns to each ethnic group one of six settlement patterns and, if relevant, provides polygons describing their location on a digital map.\n\nThe search results show that Weidmann and Gleditsch have been instrumental in developing geospatial datasets for studying international conflicts and political units, with their work spanning from 2010 through the 2010-2020 period you specified.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b7fa11c9-0474-4517-911a-e08084504750","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:18.239Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b7eb8b2d-96c5-4b49-9b74-0a98d77fb84b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"8b6c1e5e-b6e9-4ed5-b998-495d4c553e46","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013ALN9W4s6nYh8pCkqWWdXv","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"China Historical GIS CHGIS Harvard boundaries shapefile download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"CHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://chgis.fas.harvard.edu/\"},{\"title\":\"China Historical GIS | Center for Geographic Analysis\",\"url\":\"https://gis.harvard.edu/china-historical-gis\"},{\"title\":\"Historical - GIS Data Sources - LibGuides at Duke University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.duke.edu/gisdata/gisdata_historical\"},{\"title\":\"Free For All – Chinese GIS Data - Apollo Mapping\",\"url\":\"https://apollomapping.com/blog/free-chinese-gis-data\"},{\"title\":\"Hartwell China Historical GIS - CHGIS Dataverse\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/29302\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data and Maps - Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"},{\"title\":\"China Historical GIS | CGA-Legacy\",\"url\":\"https://maps.cga.harvard.edu/cga-legacy/2015/03/20/2015-03-20-china-historical-gis/\"},{\"title\":\"China GIS Data | Center for Geographic Analysis, Harvard University\",\"url\":\"https://gis2.harvard.edu/resources/data/china-gis-data\"},{\"title\":\"CHGIS V1\",\"url\":\"https://sites.fas.harvard.edu/~chgis/data/chgis/v1/\"},{\"title\":\"Project History - CHGIS - Harvard University\",\"url\":\"https://chgis.fairbank.fas.harvard.edu/pages/history/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the China Historical GIS (CHGIS) project at Harvard:\n\n## About CHGIS\n\nThe China Historical Geographic Information System, CHGIS, is a free database of placenames and historical administrative units for the Chinese Dynasties. CHGIS provides a base GIS platform for researchers to use in spatial analysis or to visualize the historical divisions of China as digital maps.\n\n## Data Contents\n\nThe data layers include nationwide coverages (for the years 1820 and 1911), and time series (for the Dynastic period from 221 BCE to 1911 CE). The administrative features include Provinces, Circuits, Prefectures, and Counties as they changed over time. Included in this dataset are also more recent shapefiles of roads, rivers, railways and even contours.\n\n## How to Download\n\nThe distribution URL is: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/chgis_v6 Additionally, a free database of place names and historical administrative units for the Chinese Dynasties can be accessed and data can be downloaded via Harvard Dataverse.\n\n## Access and Use\n\nAll CHGIS data is published for free academic use, and the contents are made available for online searching and access via XML webservice.\n\nThe main CHGIS project website is located at https://chgis.fas.harvard.edu/.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"d082f230-27f2-4778-874c-c38770d248c4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:18.776Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8b6c1e5e-b6e9-4ed5-b998-495d4c553e46","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"3d72a0e0-8ea4-4b9f-bbed-e64d768cf106","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KF32S2f8QFrQT88qCsSMq3","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"historical country codes COW Gleditsch Ward ISO mapping concordance dataset\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Country-Year State System Coding — country_year_coder • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/country_year_coder.html\"},{\"title\":\"Get, clean, and merge data\",\"url\":\"https://stats.andrewheiss.com/donors-ngo-restrictions/01_get-merge-data.html\"},{\"title\":\"Gleditsch and Ward list of independent states — gwstates • states\",\"url\":\"https://www.andybeger.com/states/reference/gwstates.html\"},{\"title\":\"A Quick Tutorial on Various State (Country) Classification Systems | Steven V. Miller\",\"url\":\"https://svmiller.com/blog/2021/01/a-tutorial-on-state-classification-systems/\"},{\"title\":\"UCDP/PRIO Armed Conflict Dataset Codebook1 Version 4-2006\",\"url\":\"https://ucdp.uu.se/downloads/replication_data/2006_c_666956-l_1-k_codebook_v4-2006b.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Two Ravens: Event Data | TwoRavens\",\"url\":\"https://tworavens.github.io/TwoRavens/EventData/\"},{\"title\":\"External Links – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/external-links/\"},{\"title\":\"UCDP/PRIO Armed Conflict Dataset Codebook1 Version 17.12\",\"url\":\"https://ucdp.uu.se/downloads/ucdpprio/ucdp-prio-acd-171.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"September 2007 Codebook for the dataset\",\"url\":\"https://ucdp.uu.se/downloads/micmilc/milc-10-codebook.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"codelist: Country Code Translation Data Frame (Cross-Sectional) in vincentarelbundock/countrycode: Convert Country Names and Country Codes\",\"url\":\"https://rdrr.io/github/vincentarelbundock/countrycode/man/codelist.html\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found comprehensive information about historical country codes and mapping systems related to COW (Correlates of War), Gleditsch and Ward, and ISO standards.\n\n## Overview of Country Code Systems\n\nThe Correlates of War (CoW) uses integers that range from 2 (the United States) to 990 (Samoa) to code states from 1816 to 2016. The Gleditsch-Ward system is a slight derivation of the CoW system, with substantial overlap and effectively the same numerical range, but important distinctions emerge as Gleditsch-Ward interpret independent states differently.\n\n## Relationship Between COW and Gleditsch & Ward\n\nGleditsch & Ward use a slightly modified version of the COW numbering system. The G&W character country code is derived from the COW character codes.\n\n## Available Code Types\n\nVarious code systems are available including cown (Correlates of War numeric), gwc (Gleditsch & Ward character), gwn (Gleditsch & Ward numeric), iso2c (ISO-2 character), iso3c (ISO-3 character), and iso3n (ISO-3 numeric).\n\n## Concordance Datasets\n\nThe countrycode package includes a reconciled dataset in panel format called codelist_panel, and for users dealing with panel data, it's recommended to \"left-merge\" their data into this panel dictionary. Additionally, GW codes and other standardizations have been mapped to the COW codes.\n\n## Resources\n\nThe Gleditsch-Ward system codes are available through their state list resource (http://privatewww.essex.ac.uk/~ksg/statelist.html), including both numeric codes (GWn) and three-letter codes (GWc).\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"6b5adb94-115b-4b9a-9b37-0e4c7ed29f5d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:19.483Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3d72a0e0-8ea4-4b9f-bbed-e64d768cf106","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
