Large Scale International Boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://catalog.data.gov/dataset/large-scale-international-boundaries\"},{\"title\":\"Large Scale International Boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://geodata.state.gov/geonetwork/srv/api/records/3bdb81a0-c1b9-439a-a0b1-85dac30c59b2\"},{\"title\":\"https://data.geodata.state.gov/waf/iso/lsib.xml\",\"url\":\"https://data.geodata.state.gov/waf/iso/lsib.xml\"},{\"title\":\"Data - Humanitarian Information Unit - Department of State\",\"url\":\"https://hiu.state.gov/data/\"},{\"title\":\"LSIB 2017: Large Scale International Boundary Polygons, Simplified | Earth Engine Data Catalog | Google for Developers\",\"url\":\"https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/USDOS_LSIB_SIMPLE_2017\"},{\"title\":\"Large Scale International Boundaries - State GeoNode\",\"url\":\"https://geonode.state.gov/layers/geonode%3ALSIB\"},{\"title\":\"Boundaries and Sovereignty Encyclopedia (BASE)\",\"url\":\"https://ggim.un.org/2unwgic/documents/Lee%20Schwartz_TP9B.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Large Scale International Boundaries (LSIB) Humanitarian Data Exchange\",\"url\":\"https://data.humdata.org/dataset/large-scale-international-boundaries-lsib\"},{\"title\":\"State\",\"url\":\"https://services.geodata.state.gov/geoserver/lsib/wms/kml?layers=lsib:LSIB\"},{\"title\":\"Global LSIB Polygons Detailed - Big Ten Academic Alliance Geoportal\",\"url\":\"https://geo.btaa.org/catalog/f199c049-2845-421b-81c8-f16eb522e689\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about the LSIB (Large Scale International Boundaries) dataset from the State Department:\n\n## Overview\nThe Office of the Geographer and Global Issues at the U.S. Department of State produces the Large Scale International Boundaries (LSIB) dataset. These data and generalized derivatives are the only international boundary lines approved for U.S. Government use.\n\n## Current Version\nThe current edition is version 11.4 (published 24 February 2025). The 11.4 release contains updated boundary lines and data refinements designed to extend the functionality of the dataset.\n\n## Data Access and Download\nThis dataset can be accessed from Geodata.state.gov, GeoPlatform.gov, or Data.gov. According to the search results, the data can also be downloaded directly from the State Department's geospatial portal.\n\n## Data Sources\nSources for these data include treaties, relevant maps, and data from boundary commissions, as well as national mapping agencies.\n\n## Historical Context\nThe Office of the Geographer and Global Issues at the U.S. Department of State releases periodic guidance bulletins to inform Federal cartographers of changes in mapping policy, mostly with regard to international boundary and sovereignty issues. This series of bulletins began in 1991, and the policies contained in them, where applicable, are reflected in the Large Scale International Boundaries (LSIB) dataset.\n\nFor direct inquiries, contact internationalboundaries@state.gov.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"39c0ffaa-805d-4b90-8342-097e8e34421c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:30.037Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5d597a4d-28b9-4077-b312-2c9adba2877c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"a210b13f-24e2-4f1e-8194-7a5f63631cf5","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SMKq3SWpUuC7v2MvCStaZe","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Vision of Britain through Time historical boundaries parish county shapefile\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Padbury Parish (CP/AP) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10217970\"},{\"title\":\"Bramley Parish (CP/Ch) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10400958/boundary\"},{\"title\":\"Keynsham Parish (AP/CP) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit_page.jsp?u_id=10435328\"},{\"title\":\"Padstow Parish (CP) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10192407/boundary\"},{\"title\":\"Wimborne Minster Parish (AP/CP) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10461844/boundary\"},{\"title\":\"Rushden Parish (CP/AP) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10280400\"},{\"title\":\"Great Britain Historical GIS - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Britain_Historical_GIS\"},{\"title\":\"Visionofbritain\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10219530/boundary\"},{\"title\":\"Crawley Parish (CP/AP/Ch) through time\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10294800\"},{\"title\":\"Boundary Map of Abbots Langley AP/CP\",\"url\":\"https://www.visionofbritain.org.uk/unit/10208889/boundary\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found:\n\nThe Great Britain Historical GIS project is currently based at the University of Portsmouth, and is the provider of the website A Vision of Britain through Time. The Great Britain Historical GIS is a spatially enabled database that documents and visualises the changing human geography of the British Isles, primarily focused on the subdivisions of the United Kingdom mainly over the 200 years since the first census in 1801.\n\nA Vision of Britain provides a vision of Britain from 1801 to now, including maps, statistical trends and historical descriptions. The system includes digital boundaries for registration districts and poor law unions (c.1840 to 1911), local government districts (1911 to 1974), and parishes (1870s to 1974).\n\nThe search results show individual parish boundary pages from the Vision of Britain website. You can access detailed boundary information and maps for specific parishes and administrative units through visionofbritain.org.uk. For accessing shapefiles or downloading boundary data in specific formats, you may need to visit the main Vision of Britain website or contact the University of Portsmouth's Historical GIS team directly, as the search results don't specify the exact methods for downloading shapefile data.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"1b9d6937-155b-4529-ac7a-4657f443efba","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:30.047Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a210b13f-24e2-4f1e-8194-7a5f63631cf5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"c0495d9f-50d1-486b-8ec5-1603f533b051","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RedQtQGENKd3Av28DmBoNG","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Chronas historical atlas open source data download geojson territories\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - jpetits/geojson: historical geojson data\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/jpetits/geojson\"},{\"title\":\"geojson.xyz\",\"url\":\"https://geojson.xyz/\"},{\"title\":\"Chronas: Enter History\",\"url\":\"https://chronas.org/\"},{\"title\":\"OpenAtlas\",\"url\":\"https://openatlas.eu/\"},{\"title\":\"Thenmap\",\"url\":\"https://www.thenmap.net/\"},{\"title\":\"World History Maps & Timelines | GeaCron World History Atlas since 3000 BC\",\"url\":\"http://geacron.com/home-en/\"},{\"title\":\"Download U.S. Files | Atlas of Historical County Boundaries Project\",\"url\":\"https://publications.newberry.org/ahcb/downloads/united_states.html\"},{\"title\":\"List of open GeoJSON datasets | Open Innovations\",\"url\":\"https://open-innovations.org/data/geojson.html\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - iamtekson/awesome-geospatial-data-sources: This is the repo for open source geospatial data download sites.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/iamtekson/awesome-geospatial-data-sources\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found related to your query:\n\n## Chronas Historical Atlas\n\nChronas.org is a history map application with over 50 million interconnected data points. However, the search results don't provide specific information about downloading GeoJSON territory data directly from Chronas.\n\n## Related Open Source Historical Territory Data\n\nIf you're looking for open source historical atlas data in GeoJSON format, here are some relevant alternatives:\n\n**Historical Basemaps Project**: This historical boundaries project aims at providing ready-to-use base maps for mapping historical data and is work in progress. All maps are stored in the geojson format: single file per feature layer, human and machine readable, easy to import in qGIS or use in D3. You can find this on GitHub (aourednik/historical-basemaps).\n\n**Thenmap**: Thenmap is a repository for historical, current and future borders, and you can use the Thenmap API to fetch geodata as GeoJSON or TopoJSON, prerendered maps as SVG files, or metadata.\n\n**Other Resources**: OpenAtlas is an open source, web based database system for complex archaeological, historical and prosopographical data.\n\nFor more detailed information about Chronas's specific data export capabilities, you may want to check their official website or documentation directly.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b8e1e11c-3f7e-45cb-ba2c-bf9591b862ae","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:30.735Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c0495d9f-50d1-486b-8ec5-1603f533b051","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"d9e6cb5d-594a-4049-811b-523ccd7ef211","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018NrjYa9iBVjMy35UCKFLVX","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"MPIDR Census Mosaic historical GIS European administrative boundaries shapefile download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"Austro_Hungarian_Empire_1910_v_1_0 (FeatureServer)\",\"url\":\"https://services8.arcgis.com/UN2BoTelitQIJWcd/ArcGIS/rest/services/Austro_Hungarian_Empire_1910_v_1_0/FeatureServer\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic data files | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/mosaic-data-files\"},{\"title\":\"International Data - GIS/Data Center Guides - Rice University Campus Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.rice.edu/confluence/display/GDCGUIDES/International+Data\"},{\"title\":\"Data by Location: International - Geographic Information Systems & Cartography - Subject Guides at American University\",\"url\":\"https://subjectguides.library.american.edu/c.php?g=175021&p=1155762\"},{\"title\":\"Data | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data\"},{\"title\":\"eu_geom: Administrative division of the European states, 1900-2003 in junkka/histmaps: Swedish historical administrative maps\",\"url\":\"https://rdrr.io/github/junkka/histmaps/man/eu_geom.html\"},{\"title\":\"Finding GIS Data: International - Maps & Geospatial: Geographic Information Systems (GIS) - Library Guides at Penn State University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.libraries.psu.edu/c.php?g=376207&p=5296088\"},{\"title\":\"Europe - International GIS Data - Guides at Penn Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.upenn.edu/c.php?g=475518&p=3254771\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about MPIDR Census Mosaic historical GIS European administrative boundaries shapefile downloads:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national and regional administrative boundaries and historical place names. Shapefiles are available for download, which refer to historical European administrative boundaries.\n\n## Available Resources\n\nThe Census Mosaic project offers several shapefile datasets for historical European boundaries:\n\n- **European States**: Providing information on the administrative division of the European states at varying geographic detail in 30-years intervals (1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003). File size is 148.41 KB.\n\n- **Austro-Hungarian Empire (1910)**: Providing information on the administrative division of the Austro-Hungarian Empire at the level of the court districts in 1910. This shapefile has been created by Helmut Rumpler and Martin Seger and been slightly modified for the MPIDR Population History GIS Collection.\n\n- **Germany**: Providing annual information on the administrative division of the German Empire at varying geographic detail across states and time (25.82 MB).\n\n- **Serbia**: Providing information on the administrative division of Serbia at the level of districts (srez). This shapefile-series has been created by Siegfried Gruber and geo-referenced by Heidi Harris for the MPIDR Population History GIS Collection.\n\n## Access Terms\n\nThe data provided can be freely used for non-commercial scientific purposes, as long as you register as a user with the Mosaic project and the source of the data is properly cited.\n\nYou can access these files through the official Census Mosaic website at **mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles** or **censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files**.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"739fe904-b45d-4556-845f-359d69594f80","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:38.956Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d9e6cb5d-594a-4049-811b-523ccd7ef211","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"389f9f01-6cc5-4d1d-a00c-b3ab9f65aab1","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01G9VtMXEMSBAb8Ee5tRfW7z","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Thenmap API historical borders GeoJSON TopoJSON countries temporal coverage\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Thenmap\",\"url\":\"https://www.thenmap.net/\"},{\"title\":\"J++ — Version 2 of the Thenmap API: Designed for humans\",\"url\":\"https://jplusplus.org/en/blog/version-two-of-the-thenmap-api/\"},{\"title\":\"Thenmap - The Historical Map Data API\",\"url\":\"https://googlemapsmania.blogspot.com/2016/01/thenmap-historical-map-data-api.html\"},{\"title\":\"Thenmap API v2\",\"url\":\"http://api.thenmap.net/doc/v2/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - topojson/world-atlas: Pre-built TopoJSON from Natural Earth.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/topojson/world-atlas\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - alexabruck/worldmap-sensitive: TopoJSON worldmap with modified country borders\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/alexabruck/worldmap-sensitive\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"Download Free GeoJSON For Every Country In The World\",\"url\":\"https://mapscaping.com/geojson-every-country-in-the-world/\"},{\"title\":\"Thenmap API v1\",\"url\":\"http://api.thenmap.net/doc/v1\"},{\"title\":\"Collection of Maps 🗺️ / Dea Bankova | Observable\",\"url\":\"https://observablehq.com/@deaxmachina/collection-of-maps\"}]\n\nHere are the search results for Thenmap API with information about historical borders, GeoJSON/TopoJSON formats, and temporal coverage:\n\n## Overview\n\nThenmap is a repository for historical, current and future borders. The Thenmap API can fetch geodata as GeoJSON or TopoJSON, prerendered maps as SVG files, or metadata.\n\n## Supported Formats\n\nUsing the API you can download historical map borders in a number of formats, including TopoJSON and GeoJSON. TopoJSON is significantly smaller, but will take longer for the API to produce.\n\n## Temporal Coverage and Date Format\n\nDates can be on the form YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, or YYYY, and the output will include only areas that existed at that very date. This means you can query historical borders for specific points in time throughout history.\n\n## API Features\n\nYou can use it to create maps with your favourite mapping tool, or fetch a ready-made SVG map. The repository contains historical administrative borders. Datasets are named like \"area-level,\" where the area uses ISO 3166 codes where available, and level uses the ten levels of administrative borders from Open Streetmap (for example, yd-4 would be the governorates of South Yemen).\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"279061d8-53c6-4691-b7d4-cffc0bd19bcd","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:50.420Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"389f9f01-6cc5-4d1d-a00c-b3ab9f65aab1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"10454867-df4b-4875-bc32-4119e10ba2e5","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018DmYPa5Z78oxCQDuzwhG9G","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"ISO 3166-3 formerly used country codes dataset CSV historical states withdrawal dates\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes/all/all.csv at master · lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes/blob/master/all/all.csv\"},{\"title\":\"ISO - Country Codes Collection\",\"url\":\"https://www.iso.org/publication/PUB500001.html\"},{\"title\":\"ISO - ISO 3166 — Country Codes\",\"url\":\"https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html\"},{\"title\":\"List of ISO 3166 country codes - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes\"},{\"title\":\"Comprehensive country codes: ISO 3166, ITU, ISO 4217 currency codes and many more\",\"url\":\"https://datahub.io/core/country-codes\"},{\"title\":\"Changes in ISO 3166-1\",\"url\":\"https://statoids.com/w3166his.html\"},{\"title\":\"ISO 3166-1:2013 - Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions — Part 1: Country codes\",\"url\":\"https://www.iso.org/standard/63545.html\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes: ISO 3166-1 country lists merged with their UN Geoscheme regional codes in ready-to-use JSON, XML, CSV data sets · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/lukes/ISO-3166-Countries-with-Regional-Codes\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - ipregistry/iso3166: Complete ISO 3166 countries and subdivisions data · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/ipregistry/iso3166\"},{\"title\":\"World countries available in multiple languages, in CSV, JSON, PHP, MySQL, MSSQL and XML formats, with associated alpha-2, alpha-3, and numeric codes as defined by the ISO 3166 standard, and with national flags included\",\"url\":\"https://stefangabos.github.io/world_countries/\"}]\n\nBased on my search results, I found several resources for ISO 3166-3 historically-used country codes:\n\n## Official ISO Resource\n\nThe ISO Country Codes Collection allows you to download the most recent, official lists of country codes including formerly used codes in CSV, XML, and XLS formats. This is available at the official ISO website under their publication PUB500001.\n\n## What ISO 3166-3 Contains\n\nISO 3166-3 defines codes for country names which have been deleted from ISO 3166-1 since its first publication in 1974. Part 3 establishes a code that represents non-current country names, for example, Yugoslavia or Czechoslovakia.\n\n## Available Datasets\n\nSeveral open-source repositories provide ISO 3166 data in CSV format:\n\n1. **Luke's ISO-3166 Repository** - Contains ISO 3166-1 country lists merged with UN Geoscheme regional codes in JSON, XML, and CSV data sets\n\n2. **DataHub's Country Codes** - Provides comprehensive country code information including ISO 3166 codes and currency codes\n\n3. **IP Registry's ISO 3166 Repository** - Contains the full list of ISO 3166-1 countries and ISO 3166-2 subdivisions in CSV text files\n\n4. **Statoids.com** - Provides a detailed change history for ISO 3166-1 with specific dates and withdrawal information\n\nFor the most authoritative and up-to-date dataset with withdrawal dates, the official ISO Country Codes Collection is recommended as it's maintained by the ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"cc832cfe-a7e6-417f-b1a9-633391c69c2f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:51.849Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"10454867-df4b-4875-bc32-4119e10ba2e5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

acdh-oeaw/OpenAtlas: OpenAtlas is an open source, web based database system for complex archaeological, historical and prosopographical data.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/acdh-oeaw/OpenAtlas\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - craws/OpenAtlas: OpenAtlas is an open source, web based database system for complex archaeological, historical and prosopographical data. · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/craws/OpenAtlas\"},{\"title\":\"OpenAtlas | Tempopedia\",\"url\":\"https://www.tempopedia.org/directory/openatlas\"},{\"title\":\"github.com-craws-OpenAtlas_-_2021-06-25_15-15-38 : craws : Free Download, Borrow, and Streaming : Internet Archive\",\"url\":\"https://archive.org/details/github.com-craws-OpenAtlas_-_2021-06-25_15-15-38\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - stefaneichert/OpenAtlas: OpenAtlas is an open-source application for archeological, historical and geospatial data.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/stefaneichert/OpenAtlas\"},{\"title\":\"OpenAtlas | CLARIAH-AT\",\"url\":\"https://clariah.at/en/projects/openatlas/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) OpenAtlas: An Open-Source Application to Map Historical Data with CIDOC CRM\",\"url\":\"https://www.academia.edu/126013751/OpenAtlas_An_Open_Source_Application_to_Map_Historical_Data_with_CIDOC_CRM\"},{\"title\":\"OpenATLAS – an open source database application for archaeological, historical and spatial data | CHNT Archiv\",\"url\":\"https://archiv.chnt.at/openatlas/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - stefaneichert/OpenAtlas-Alpha-Version: OpenATLAS is a database application for the work with archeological, historical and spatial data.\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/stefaneichert/OpenAtlas-Alpha-Version\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about OpenAtlas:\n\n## Overview\nOpenAtlas is an open source, web based database system for complex archaeological, historical and prosopographical data. It is designed to acquire, edit and manage research data from various fields of humanities like history, archaeology and cultural heritage as well as related scientific data.\n\n## Key Features\nThe system provides a user interface that can be accessed via any common web-browser and allows researchers to work with their data via customized forms and categories to record complex relations. The information is mapped as a network using classes and properties of the CIDOC CRM.\n\n## Availability and Download\nThe software is available through multiple sources:\n- **Official website**: https://openatlas.eu/\n- **GitHub repositories**: The code is hosted on GitHub (notably at https://github.com/acdh-oeaw/OpenAtlas and https://github.com/craws/OpenAtlas)\n- The code is licensed under the GNU General Public License Version 2, June 1991.\n\nInstallation instructions are available in the install.md file in the repository.\n\nFor specific download links and the latest version, I recommend visiting the official OpenAtlas website or the GitHub repositories where you can access the source code directly.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"0864a010-b2af-463d-934b-81d28aad7e8e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:52.130Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c47c79f1-bf38-4b0b-bce3-bff966929028","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"9c1795f8-c17c-470c-b339-9b2bd201ba0b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01F6QXzsijXppHvnHWFoQrz6","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"democracyData countrycode R package historical state system concordance COW GW ISO\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Country-Year State System Coding — country_year_coder • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/country_year_coder.html\"},{\"title\":\"democracyData/README.Rmd at master · xmarquez/democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/xmarquez/democracyData/blob/master/README.Rmd\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - xmarquez/democracyData: Access and manipulate most standard scholarly measures of democracy\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/xmarquez/democracyData\"},{\"title\":\"Access and Manipulate Most Available Democracy Datasets • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/\"},{\"title\":\"democracyData/R/country_year_coder.R at master · xmarquez/democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/xmarquez/democracyData/blob/master/R/country_year_coder.R\"},{\"title\":\"Deep Clean: Varieties of Democracy (V-Dem) | DANTE Project\",\"url\":\"https://www.dante-project.org/vignettes/clean-vdem\"},{\"title\":\"democracyData/R/redownload_functions.R at master · xmarquez/democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/xmarquez/democracyData/blob/master/R/redownload_functions.R\"},{\"title\":\"Generate a data frame containing all the democracy measures accessible from this package — generate_democracy_scores_dataset • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/generate_democracy_scores_dataset.html\"},{\"title\":\"Pre-Processing the Varieties of Democracy Dataset • demcon\",\"url\":\"https://dante-sttr.gitlab.io/demcon/articles/clean-vdem.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download democracy data with democracyData package in R – R Functions and Packages for Political Science Analysis\",\"url\":\"https://rforpoliticalscience.com/2022/03/18/download-democracy-data-with-democracydata-pacakge-in-r/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found comprehensive information about the democracyData R package and its country code concordance functions. Here's what I found:\n\n## Key Information About democracyData Package\n\nThe democracyData package archives a large number of datasets measuring democracy in use in the scholarly literature, and it provides functions to access many others.\n\n## Country-Year Coder Function\n\nThe first convenience function is `country_year_coder`, which works like the `countrycode` package, except that it is able to determine state system information for country-year pairs. It also tries to figure out whether a given country-year is in the specific state system list.\n\n## State System Codes Supported\n\nThe package handles multiple historical state system concordances:\n\n- \"GWn\": Numeric code for the country in the Gleditsch-Ward system of states\n- \"GWc\": Three letter code for the country in the Gleditsch-Ward system of states\n- COW (Correlates of War) codes\n\nThe Gleditsch-Ward system is very similar, but not identical, to the Correlates of War and Polity codes.\n\n## Extended Codes\n\n\"extended_GWn\": Identical to GWn, but includes a number of not commonly used codes for units of doubtful sovereignty (751 for Hyderabad before its incorporation into India, 666.001 and 666.002 for Israel pre 1967 borders and occupied territories, and 605 for Western Sahara).\n\nThe package includes variables like `GWn`, `cown`, and `in_GW_system` to help with state system coding and concordance across different time periods and country name variations.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"63c59677-b00f-4b55-b7de-caf6a6f7cac0","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:35:55.153Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9c1795f8-c17c-470c-b339-9b2bd201ba0b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"d20bd369-81b3-4dcf-8027-628956cafacd","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"progress","data":{"type":"search_results_received","resultCount":10,"query":"COW territorial change dataset v6 CSV download 1816 2018"},"toolUseID":"srvtoolu_011FWWZ6uwGiwghBLUaGe4Gd","parentToolUseID":"toolu_016d4MLpcA5AnvCq6Gxt5Fea","uuid":"6d9a45df-e299-492f-afb3-63799478e167","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:03.803Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"42deff04-cb3d-4276-9549-45716eee2480","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ad8bcd3bK9tNcGMJnXWxYH","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"GeoEPR ethnic power relations geospatial dataset ETH Zurich download shapefile\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Ethnic Power Relations (EPR) Dataset Family\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - GeoEPR 2021\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/geoepr/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - GeoEPR v.1.0\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/geoepr/v1.0/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Geo-referencing of Ethnic Groups\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/greg/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - GeoEPR-ETH Version 2.0\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/geoepr/v2.0/\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - GeoEPR 2018\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/geoepr/2018.html\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - Data\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/\"},{\"title\":\"GeoEPR - Geo-referencing Ethnic Power Relations - Big Ten Academic Alliance Geoportal\",\"url\":\"https://geo.btaa.org/catalog/a16cbecd-a669-416b-9c74-4db5acc8143a\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - GeoEPR 2023\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/geoepr/beta.html\"},{\"title\":\"The Ethnic Power Relations Dataset Family\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/publications/vogt2015integrating.pdf\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the GeoEPR dataset:\n\n## GeoEPR Dataset Overview\n\nThe GeoEPR dataset provides geo-spatial information about every politically relevant ethnic group. It assigns to each ethnic group one of six settlement patterns and, if relevant, provides polygons describing their location on a digital map.\n\n## Download Options\n\nThe latest version (2021, released on June 8, 2021) of the raw EPR component datasets can be downloaded directly in CSV (UTF-8 encoded), JSON, Excel (.xls), Excel 2007 OOXML (.xlsx), Shapefile, and SQL formats.\n\nFor the most recent version, the complete GeoEPR-ETH dataset can be downloaded as GeoEPR-ETH_v.2.0.zip in ESRI Shapefile format, with files compressed into a *.zip folder.\n\n## Coordinate System\n\nThe GeoEPR polygons follow the WGS84 coordinate system (SRID 4326).\n\n## Source Location\n\nThe datasets are hosted at ETH Zurich's International Conflict Research group at https://icr.ethz.ch/data/epr/, which provides access to multiple versions and formats of the GeoEPR dataset for download.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"a3355953-29e5-46ae-8e77-98453481ea75","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:06.733Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"42deff04-cb3d-4276-9549-45716eee2480","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"bbe32b99-5c59-4273-b12a-7ebcedf5f574","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016d4MLpcA5AnvCq6Gxt5Fea","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"COW territorial change dataset v6 CSV download 1816 2018\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Territorial Change (v6) – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/territorial-change/\"},{\"title\":\"Data Sets – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/\"},{\"title\":\"COW War Data, 1816 – 2007 (v4.0) – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/cow-war/\"},{\"title\":\"Correlates of War - Datasets - the Datahub\",\"url\":\"https://old.datahub.io/dataset/cow\"},{\"title\":\"Correlates of War - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Correlates_of_War\"},{\"title\":\"News – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/news/\"},{\"title\":\"National Material Capabilities (v6.0) – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/national-material-capabilities/\"},{\"title\":\"Data Download – International Conflict Data Project\",\"url\":\"https://internationalconflict.ua.edu/data-download/\"},{\"title\":\"Correlates of War Project | re3data.org\",\"url\":\"https://www.re3data.org/repository/r3d100012250\"},{\"title\":\"Direct Contiguity (v3.2) – The Correlates of War Project\",\"url\":\"https://correlatesofwar.org/data-sets/direct-contiguity/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found information about the COW Territorial Change dataset v6:\n\n## COW Territorial Change Dataset v6 (1816-2018)\n\nThe territorial change dataset is the result of the effort to identify and code all territorial changes involving at least one nation-state (as defined by the Correlates of War project) for the period 1816-2018.\n\n### Availability and Format\n\nOn March 25, 2019 the sixth version of this data set (that covers 1816-2018) was released. The revised territorial change data – now version 6 – is available in .csv format, with a file named tc2018.csv: the data in comma-separated variable ASCII format.\n\n### Files Included\n\nThe ZIP file contains the following files: tc2018.csv: the data in comma-separated variable ASCII format; tc2018.xls: the data in a Microsoft spreadsheet format; and tcmanual.pdf: The main territorial change coding manual.\n\n### Data Content\n\nThis dataset contains information on the entities involved, the gaining side, the losing side, the territory exchanged, as well as the type and date of the exchange.\n\n### Access\n\nThe dataset is hosted at the Correlates of War Project website (correlatesofwar.org), specifically at the Territorial Change data page. The territorial change data set is hosted by Steven Miller, Clemson University, under the COW Data Hosting Program. In case of questions or concerns concerning the data or coding rules, contact the host by email at svmille@clemson.edu.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"fba94367-7131-4b54-a39b-51924fa54675","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:08.239Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"bbe32b99-5c59-4273-b12a-7ebcedf5f574","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"9b8e2b9d-af18-4ec9-9cab-220aef6fc73c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KVitquUocH49Atzz2eGtQE","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"V-Dem Varieties of Democracy dataset historical countries territory sovereignty\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"V-Dem Democracy Indices - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V-Dem_Democracy_Indices\"},{\"title\":\"V-Dem\",\"url\":\"https://www.v-dem.net/\"},{\"title\":\"The 'Varieties of Democracy' data: how do researchers ...\",\"url\":\"https://ourworldindata.org/vdem-electoral-democracy-data\"},{\"title\":\"Simplified version of the V-Dem version 14 dataset — vdem_simple • democracyData\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/democracyData/reference/vdem_simple.html\"},{\"title\":\"Varieties of Democracy Project | Kellogg Institute For International Studies\",\"url\":\"https://kellogg.nd.edu/research/major-research-initiatives/varieties-democracy-project\"},{\"title\":\"The V-Dem Dataset – V-Dem\",\"url\":\"https://www.v-dem.net/data/the-v-dem-dataset/\"},{\"title\":\"Historical V-Dem – V-Dem\",\"url\":\"https://www.v-dem.net/about/historical-v-dem/\"},{\"title\":\"Contains the Varieties of Democracy (V-Dem) Dataset v. 11.1 • vdem\",\"url\":\"https://xmarquez.github.io/vdem/\"},{\"title\":\"Varieties of Democracy (V-Dem) | University of Gothenburg\",\"url\":\"https://www.gu.se/en/research/varieties-of-democracy-v-dem\"},{\"title\":\"Datasets – V-Dem\",\"url\":\"https://www.v-dem.net/data/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's comprehensive information about the V-Dem Varieties of Democracy dataset regarding historical countries, territory, and sovereignty:\n\n## Dataset Coverage and Historical Scope\n\nV-Dem utilizes historical data dating back to the 1800s for some countries, multiple coders for each question, and large measurement models. More specifically, as of version 15 of the dataset, V-Dem covers 202 countries, going back in time as far as 1789.\n\n## Country Definition and Sovereignty\n\nThe dataset has specific criteria for what constitutes a country unit: A V-Dem country is a political unit enjoying at least some degree of functional and/or formal sovereignty. V-Dem country coding starts in 1789, or from when a country first enjoyed at least some degree of functional and/or formal sovereignty.\n\n## Historical Entity Tracking\n\nV-Dem tracks how countries have changed over time: Many countries go by different names in different time-periods, for example due to name changes, changes in territory, colonization, occupation, or independence.\n\n## Coverage of Historical Territories\n\nMany countries have been covered since 1900, including before they became independent from their colonial powers. Historical V-Dem covers about 80 polities and extends almost 200 V-Dem indicators as far back as 1789, including about 130 of V-Dem's expert-coded indicators. These include all major countries with continuous lineages as independent states from the 18th or 19th centuries, but also some major colonies such as India and Indonesia. Historical V-Dem also contains several extra, historical polities that have ceased to exist, such as Bavaria and the Two Sicilies.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e822bd09-a4c1-42a9-bb8f-4a49fd19bb9a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:08.878Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9b8e2b9d-af18-4ec9-9cab-220aef6fc73c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"9cfafce7-a208-40bd-9fe6-ea622fbd82ea","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014gZqWbhpp1sKy6wcsZ2hHY","type":"tool_result","content":"# Clio Infra: Datasets and Indicators Overview\n\nClio Infra provides **86 datasets** covering **224 countries** with historical data spanning from 1500 to 2018. The platform organizes indicators into these major categories:\n\n## Primary Data Categories\n\n**Agriculture**: Livestock per capita (cattle, goats, pigs, sheep), cropland, and pasture metrics\n\n**Demography**: Population statistics, life expectancy, infant mortality, height measurements, poverty, and hunger data\n\n**Environment**: Biodiversity, CO2 and SO2 emissions\n\n**Finance**: Exchange rates, gold standard adoption, government bond yields, and debt levels\n\n**Gender Equality**: Numeracy parity, education equality, inheritance laws, and parliamentary representation\n\n**Human Capital**: Education years, book production, educational inequality, numeracy, and universities founded\n\n**Institutions**: Armed conflicts, executive recruitment competitiveness, homicide rates, democracy indices, and political participation measures\n\n**Labour Relations**: Strike data, labour disputes, and manufacturing work weeks\n\n**National Accounts**: GDP per capita and social spending\n\n**Prices and Wages**: Income inequality, inflation, real wages, and wealth distribution\n\n**Production**: Mineral extraction data (12 commodities)\n\n## Territorial Coverage\n\nThe database explicitly notes data uses \"modern 2012 boundaries\" with separate historical boundary datasets available, addressing political entity and border changes across time periods."}]},"uuid":"940be4bc-a583-4673-85de-1ffc70f9a19e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:21.731Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9cfafce7-a208-40bd-9fe6-ea622fbd82ea","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"a2216a27-0c21-45e3-825b-584173147e03","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01F9F5YghAEF52AttJs8DMkA","type":"tool_result","content":"# Thenmap Overview\n\n## What is Thenmap?\n\nThenmap is \"a repository for historical, current and future borders.\" It enables users to create custom maps or obtain pre-made SVG files showing territorial boundaries across different time periods.\n\n## Temporal Coverage\n\nThe platform provides geospatial data spanning from the mid-20th century onward, with specific datasets covering different periods based on region.\n\n## Available Datasets and Date Ranges\n\nThe API currently includes these geographic datasets:\n\n- **World borders**: 1945–present\n- **Swedish municipalities**: 1974–present\n- **Swedish counties**: 1968–present\n- **Finnish municipalities**: 2010–present\n- **US states**: 1865–present\n- **Norwegian municipalities**: 2006–present\n- **Norwegian counties**: added 2018\n- **Greenland municipalities**: 1979–present\n- **Swiss municipalities**: 2010–present\n- **Danish municipalities**: added 2017\n\n## Data Format Options\n\nUsers can access data in multiple formats:\n\n- **GeoJSON and TopoJSON** (via API)\n- **SVG files** (pre-rendered maps or custom)\n- **Metadata** (integrated with WikiData)\n\nThe platform also offers a lightweight JavaScript library (Thenmap.js) and a Python library for easier integration."}]},"uuid":"7e531373-35f7-4a60-b353-43d6b6f1a339","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:23.132Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a2216a27-0c21-45e3-825b-584173147e03","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"da24b599-7c1b-47ef-ac15-1aa19dcaf230","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MWCH6u8pCpqNuBt74QuQer","type":"tool_result","content":"# Response\n\nThe webpage content provided does not contain information about a HIPE (Historical International Political Economy) dataset. The page describes a scholarly chapter titled \"Historical International Political Economy\" by J. Morrison, published in 2021 in The Oxford Handbook of International Political Economy.\n\nThe chapter discusses \"the field's major modes of engaging history: holistic, comparative, and deep\" and explores how International Political Economy scholars approach historical analysis, but it does not describe or reference a dataset with specific data contents, temporal/spatial coverage, format specifications, or country code information.\n\nTo answer your questions about the HIPE dataset's structure, coverage, and technical specifications, you would need to consult documentation specific to that dataset itself, which is not available in the provided content."}]},"uuid":"7048a71f-f1b6-4419-8c3f-17664531d5ca","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:39.391Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"da24b599-7c1b-47ef-ac15-1aa19dcaf230","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"34444d27-65a1-4294-97e5-f714f8fa54c1","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012eLBBR21dpfPtBKWFGanaE","type":"tool_result","content":"# Historical Basemaps Project Overview\n\n## Available Formats\n\nThe project provides georeferenced boundary data in three primary formats:\n\n1. **GeoJSON** - The primary format stored in the `/geojson` directory, supporting use in D3, Leaflet, qGIS, and OpenLayers\n2. **SVG** - Converted from GeoJSON files using an R script with Natural Earth projection by default\n3. **JSON Index** - A lightweight `index.json` file listing available years and countries for quick querying\n\n## Time Period Coverage\n\nThe README indicates the collection spans extensive historical periods, with specific examples mentioning \"1492\" and \"1938\" as available snapshot years. The project aims to cover \"global or continental scale\" historical data mapping, though the exact complete date range isn't exhaustively listed in the visible content.\n\n## Data Structure\n\nEach GeoJSON file contains polygon multifeatures with properties including:\n- NAME (country/region identifier)\n- SUBJECTO (colonial authority or regional designation)\n- PARTOF (larger cultural grouping)\n- BORDERPRECISION (ordinal values 1-3 indicating accuracy level)\n\nA separate `places.geojson` file contains settlement locations with temporal fields (`inhabitedSince`, `inhabitedUntil`).\n\n## Repository Statistics\n\nThe project contains **264 commits** across its development history, with **686 stars** and **124 forks** on GitHub, indicating active community engagement with this historical geographic dataset."}]},"uuid":"44e1f51d-6f21-4468-8dfb-8f633f8320d9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:43.315Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"34444d27-65a1-4294-97e5-f714f8fa54c1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"6ef73016-6092-43ef-b6da-510dbf54f7f0","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"e4b3ce19-89dd-409d-b0c7-935fedb62227","agentId":"a2af46c92d818d1a3","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PQs8dU7ARvrNvMC2Vbwiqg","type":"tool_result","content":"# ETH Zurich International Conflict Research Datasets\n\nBased on the webpage content, here are the available datasets:\n\n## **GROWup Platform**\nA unified relational database combining ethnic groups and intrastate conflict datasets. Accessible through three portals:\n- Public Front-End (visualization)\n- EPR Atlas (documentation)\n- Research Front-End (customizable downloads in country-year or group-year format)\n\n## **EPR Dataset Family 2021**\n**Temporal Coverage:** 1946-2021\n\nComponent datasets include:\n- **EPR Core Dataset**: Identifies politically relevant ethnic groups and state power access\n- **GeoEPR**: Adds geospatial information\n- **ACD2EPR**: Links conflict to ethnicity\n- **EPR-TEK**: Covers transnational ethnic groups\n- **EPR-ER**: Documents refugee ethnic composition\n- **EPR-ED**: Records linguistic, religious, and racial cleavages\n\n## **CShapes 2.0**\n**Focus:** Historical state boundaries and capitals\n\n**Temporal Coverage:** Over 60 years of history on \"borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories\"\n\n**Access Methods:** Interactive visualization or R package (cshapes)\n\n## **Other Datasets**\nThe page references additional datasets authored by group members, accessible through a separate link."}]},"uuid":"ac715054-1750-4324-ab4f-e1ff75432df8","timestamp":"2026-03-24T20:36:45.613Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"6ef73016-6092-43ef-b6da-510dbf54f7f0","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
